reforms.description: The 1999 reform of the taxe professionnelle aims to phase out the wage share of the tax base, which is expected to reduce labor costs significantly and create approximately 20,000 jobs in 2000.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By reducing labor costs, the reform is expected to enhance employment opportunities and stimulate economic activity.
reforms.status_evidence: The reform is designed to reduce labor costs and has been announced as part of the government's fiscal strategy.
reforms.importance_rationale: The reform represents a significant adjustment in tax policy aimed at reducing labor costs, which is expected to have a meaningful impact on employment.
reforms.source_quote: The 1999 reform of the taxe professionnelle, designed to phase out the wage share of the tax base, is reducing labour cost.

reforms.description: The application of a reduced rate of VAT for domestic work and home services, initiated at the end of 1999, is expected to create several thousand jobs and reduce unrecorded employment.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By lowering the cost of domestic services, the reform encourages formal employment and job creation in the sector.
reforms.status_evidence: The reform has been applied since the end of 1999 to promote job creation in domestic services.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform is a significant policy change aimed at enhancing employment in the domestic services sector, with expected positive economic impacts.
reforms.source_quote: The application of the reduced rate of VAT for domestic work and home services to individuals since the end of 1999 should permit the creation of several thousand jobs.

reforms.description: In 1999, France initiated pilot experiments with performance contracts aimed at enhancing public sector efficiency. These contracts allow government agencies to manage budgetary appropriations flexibly while committing to performance targets defined by public service user expectations.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By linking budget resources to performance outcomes, the initiative aims to improve efficiency and productivity in the public sector, which can positively impact overall economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The pilot experiments with performance contracts were initiated in 1999.
reforms.importance_rationale: The introduction of performance contracts represents a significant shift towards a more results-oriented public sector management approach, which can lead to improved efficiency and effectiveness in government operations.
reforms.source_quote: In France, an approach of this type was initiated in 1999, in the form of pilot experiments with performance contracts.

reforms.description: The French government introduced the 35-hour workweek law, which aims to reduce working hours for employees in order to create jobs and improve productivity.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By reducing working hours, the initiative is expected to create jobs and enhance productivity, thereby supporting economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The law was enacted and began to take effect on 1 February 2000.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant structural change in labor policy aimed at improving employment rates and productivity across various sectors.
reforms.source_quote: The law set the statutory working week at 35 hours on 1 January 2000 for enterprises with more than 20 employees.

reforms.description: The French government is continuing its policy of simplifying legislation and reducing administrative formalities for small businesses, which includes tax exemptions and the creation of an Internet portal for business registration.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By reducing administrative burdens, the reform aims to foster entrepreneurship and facilitate the creation of new businesses, which can enhance economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The government is continuing its policy of simplifying legislation and reducing administrative formalities.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant structural change aimed at improving the entrepreneurial climate, which is crucial for economic recovery and growth.
reforms.source_quote: The government’s policy of simplifying legislation and reducing administrative formalities... is being continued in 2000 with a further wave of taxes being scrapped.

reforms.description: The French authorities announced a new set of measures to facilitate starting a business, including the abolition of registration taxes and the creation of an Internet portal for entrepreneurs.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: These measures aim to lower the barriers to entry for new businesses, thereby encouraging entrepreneurship and innovation.
reforms.status_evidence: In April 2000 the authorities announced a new set of measures to facilitate starting a business.
reforms.importance_rationale: While significant, these measures are part of a broader initiative to improve the business environment rather than a standalone major reform.
reforms.source_quote: In April 2000 the authorities announced a new set of measures to facilitate starting a business.

reforms.description: The law on innovation and research of 12 July 1999 aims to encourage the transfer of technology from the public research sector to the private sector and the creation of innovative companies. It includes measures for researcher mobility, collaboration between public and corporate research, and adjustments to the tax and legal framework for innovative enterprises.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Facilitates technology transfer and enhances collaboration between sectors, which can lead to increased innovation and productivity.
reforms.status_evidence: The law was enacted to streamline support for research and innovation.
reforms.importance_rationale: This law represents a significant structural change in the innovation landscape, aiming to enhance collaboration and technology transfer, which are crucial for economic growth.
reforms.source_quote: The law on innovation and research of 12 July 1999 should encourage the transfer of technology from the public research sector to the private sector, and the creation of innovative companies.

reforms.description: The regulations for mutual funds investing in innovative companies have been improved, allowing these funds to invest in any company approved by the National Agency for the Promotion of Research (ANVAR), thus enhancing financial support for innovative firms.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By facilitating investment in innovative firms, this reform is expected to enhance the availability of capital for startups and promote innovation.
reforms.status_evidence: The regulations have been improved to allow investment in any innovative firm.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant change in the regulatory framework for investment in innovation, potentially increasing funding for innovative companies.
reforms.source_quote: The FCPI regulations have been improved to allow investment in any innovative firm.

reforms.description: The legal framework for simplified joint stock companies has been extended to allow any innovative firm to incorporate as a simplified joint stock company, facilitating easier business formation.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By simplifying the incorporation process for innovative firms, this reform encourages entrepreneurship and the establishment of new businesses.
reforms.status_evidence: The regulations applicable to simplified joint stock companies were extended to allow any innovative firm to incorporate.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform significantly eases the process for innovative firms to establish themselves, which is crucial for fostering a vibrant startup ecosystem.
reforms.source_quote: The regulations applicable to simplified joint stock companies were extended to allow any innovative firm to incorporate.

reforms.description: The French government enacted the 'Loi sur l'innovation et la recherche', which aims to enhance the framework for innovation and research activities in the country.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: This law is expected to stimulate R&D activities and foster collaboration between public and private sectors, enhancing overall innovation capacity.
reforms.status_evidence: The law was officially published and is in effect.
reforms.importance_rationale: This law represents a significant overhaul of the innovation and research landscape in France, aiming to create a more conducive environment for R&D and collaboration.
reforms.source_quote: Ministère de l'Éducation nationale, de la Recherche et de la Technologie (2000), La loi sur l'innovation et la recherche.

reforms.description: The Council of Ministers adopted a bill on innovation aimed at encouraging enterprise creation by government researchers and simplifying interactions between research bodies and businesses.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Facilitates the creation of new technology firms, enhancing innovation and economic dynamism.
reforms.status_evidence: The bill was adopted by the Council of Ministers.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant structural change in how government researchers interact with the business sector, promoting innovation and enterprise creation.
reforms.source_quote: The Council of Ministers adopts the bill on innovation, which is designed to encourage enterprise creation by government researchers and to simplify relations between research bodies and the world of business.

reforms.description: The government announced a set of measures worth FF 200 million to facilitate the creation of technology firms.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: These measures are intended to stimulate the establishment of new technology firms, which can drive innovation and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The government announces the launch of a set of measures, worth FF 200 million, designed to facilitate the creation of technology firms.
reforms.importance_rationale: While significant, these measures are part of a broader strategy and do not represent a complete overhaul of the innovation system.
reforms.source_quote: The government announces the launch of a set of measures, worth FF 200 million, designed to facilitate the creation of technology firms.
